name: Bhai Nanak Singh (Nabha)
body: Bhai Nanak Singh Ji and his brother, Bhai Babu Singh Ji hailed from the city of Nabha in the Patiala district of Punjab. Both brothers were immensely knowledgeable about Sikh teachings and their style of Kirtan was rooted in tradition, not often heard these days.

According to Veer Satnam Singh Virdy from Zambia, Bhai Nanak Singh Ji served as a ‘Granthi’ in the Gurdwara Sahib in Lusaka from 1982 to 1988 and upon completing his contract, he returned to India. During his stay in Zambia, Bhai Nanak Singh Ji taught Kirtan to a number of children in the Sikh community. He also started Veer Satnam Singh Virdy with Kirtan training as a child, and after he left for India, Veer Satnam Singh traveled to Kenya to continue learning from Bhai Raghbir Singh Ji Diwana.

Upon his return to India, Bhai Nanak Singh Ji was accompanied by Principal Gursharan Singh Ji on tabla. According to him, Bhai Nanak Singh Ji was a great fan of Giani Man Singh Jhor and used to love listening to Giani Ji’s lectures.
If anyone has more recordings or information about Bhai Nanak Singh Ji, please contact us.
